recipe.description: Onions, cream cheese and sour cream are good additions
recipe.notes: Tags: Main, Normal, chicken thighs, artichoke hearts, sun dried tomatoes
recipe.title: Crock Pot chicken thighs

ingredient_lines.raw_text: INGREDIENTS
Chicken thighs
Seasonings
Jarred Artichoke Hearts + liquid
Julienne Cut Sun-Dried Tomatoes
Garlic

ingredient_lines.input_item: HOW TO MAKE THIS CROCK POT CHICKEN THIGHS RECIPE
ingredient_lines.raw_text: HOW TO MAKE THIS CROCK POT CHICKEN THIGHS RECIPE:

ingredient_lines.note: Start by spraying a 6 quart crock pot
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Start by spraying a 6-quart crock pot/slow cooker with cooking spray.

ingredient_lines.input_item: Next, season chicken thighs with salt dried oregano.
ingredient_lines.note: pepper, Transfer chicken thighs to the slow cooker in one layer.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Next, season chicken thighs with salt, pepper, and dried oregano. Transfer chicken thighs to the slow cooker in one layer.

ingredient_lines.note: layer the artichoke hearts over the chicken and sprinkle with garlic. Take a 1/3 cup of the liquid from the jar with the artichoke hearts and pour it over the top.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Then, layer the artichoke hearts over the chicken and sprinkle with garlic. Take a 1/3-cup of the liquid from the jar with the artichoke hearts and pour it over the top.

ingredient_lines.note: cover; cook on HIGH for 4-4-#1$2 hours, or on LOW for about 6 hours.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Next, cover; cook on HIGH for 4 to 4-1/2 hours, or on LOW for about 6 hours.

ingredient_lines.input_item: Add in the sun-dried tomatoes 30 minutes before chicken is done cooking.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Add in the sun-dried tomatoes 30 minutes before chicken is done cooking.

ingredient_lines.preparation: sprinkle with fresh parsley and serve.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Finally, sprinkle with fresh parsley and serve.
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
CAN I USE CHICKEN BREASTS INSTEAD?

ingredient_lines.note: you can absolutely use chicken breasts, but keep in mind that chicken breasts will need less time cooking, so start checking around 5-hour mark if cooking on LOW, and around the 3-hour mark if cooking on HIGH.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Yes, you can absolutely use chicken breasts, but keep in mind that chicken breasts will need less time cooking, so start checking around 5-hour mark if cooking on LOW, and around the 3-hour mark if cooking on HIGH.
CAN I USE BONE-IN CHICKEN THIGHS?

ingredient_lines.note: using bone-in chicken thighs is fine. They might take a bit longer to cook
ingredient_lines.preparation: about 20 minutes longer in the crock pot.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Yes, using bone-in chicken thighs is fine. They might take a bit longer to cook; about 20 minutes longer in the crock pot.
CAN I DOUBLE THE RECIPE?

ingredient_lines.note: I think up to 6 is a good number, maybe even 8, depending on their size, but anything more than that might get a bit crowded. You want them all in one layer
ingredient_lines.preparation: always read not to use too many pieces of meat in a slow cooker., slightly overlapping so to make room for the rest of the ingredients.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: I have always read not to use too many pieces of meat in a slow cooker. I think up to 6 is a good number, maybe even 8, depending on their size, but anything more than that might get a bit crowded. You want them all in one layer, slightly overlapping so to make room for the rest of the ingredients.

ingredient_lines.input_item: HOW TO STORE CROCK POT CHICKEN THIGHS
ingredient_lines.raw_text: HOW TO STORE CROCK POT CHICKEN THIGHS

ingredient_lines.input_item: Your crock pot chicken thighs should
ingredient_lines.preparation: be cooled and refrigerated 2 hours after cooking.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Your crock pot chicken thighs should be cooled and refrigerated 2 hours after cooking.

ingredient_lines.input_item: Keep them stored in your fridge
ingredient_lines.note: in an airtight container for 3-4 days.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Keep them stored in your fridge in an airtight container for 3 to 4 days.

ingredient_lines.note: You can also freeze them. Let the chicken thighs cool completely; transfer to a freezer bag and place in the freezer. Keep frozen for up to 3 months.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: You can also freeze them. Let the chicken thighs cool completely; transfer to a freezer bag and place in the freezer. Keep frozen for up to 3 months.
HOW TO REHEAT CHICKEN THIGHS

ingredient_lines.input_item: Place chicken thighs on a baking sheet and reheat
ingredient_lines.note: in a 350˚F oven for 15-20 minutes, or until heated through.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Place chicken thighs on a baking sheet and reheat in a 350˚F oven for 15 to 20 minutes, or until heated through.
TIPS FOR USING YOUR CROCKPOT

ingredient_lines.note: Use the right size crock pot.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Use the right size crock pot.

ingredient_lines.input_item: Coating the inside of the crock pot with a non-stick spray
ingredient_lines.note: can prevent the need for scrubbing later.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Coating the inside of the crock pot with a non-stick spray can prevent the need for scrubbing later.

ingredient_lines.note: For most crock pots, the high setting is about 280˚F, and the low setting is around 170˚F.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: For most crock pots, the high setting is about 280˚F, and the low setting is around 170˚F.

ingredient_lines.note: Cooking on HIGH for one hour is about equal to cooking on the low setting for two hours.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Cooking on HIGH for one hour is about equal to cooking on the low setting for two hours.

ingredient_lines.note: at the end to keep flavors bright and fresh.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Add fresh herbs at the end to keep flavors bright and fresh.

ingredient_lines.note: like potatoes, carrots, and other root vegetables at the bottom of the slow cooker where they will have more moisture and cook thoroughly.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Place hard vegetables, like potatoes, carrots, and other root vegetables at the bottom of the slow cooker where they will have more moisture and cook thoroughly.

ingredient_lines.input_item: Use cheaper cuts of meat. Inexpensive cuts of meat are often high in fat
ingredient_lines.note: both of which break down during long hour cooking methods.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Use cheaper cuts of meat. Inexpensive cuts of meat are often high in fat or connective tissue, both of which break down during long hour cooking methods.

ingredient_lines.note: Add dairy at the end., and other dairy products can break down and coagulate if overheated. Stir the dairy products into, so it has just enough time to heat through.
ingredient_lines.preparation: crock pot in the last 15-30 minutes
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Add dairy at the end. Milk, cream, and other dairy products can break down and coagulate if overheated. Stir the dairy products into crock pot in the last 15-30 minutes, so it has just enough time to heat through.
